California
Fair P01itical
Practices Commission
september L7, L99].
Patricio I'Patrt Miranda
Councilmember
city of Irwindale
505o N. Irwindale Avenue
IrwindaLe, CA 917 07
Re: Your Request for Advice
our FiIe No. A-91-3 7 2
Dear Mr. Miranda:
You have requested advice concerning application of the
conf I ict-of-interest 
provisions of the PoIitical Reform Act (the
rrActrr) 1 to your duties as a city councilmember in the city of
Irwindale. ihe following advice is based on the facts provided in
your letter, as well as our conference call on August 29, L991,
witfr ur. Charles llartin, the city Attorney.
OUESTI ON
Under the Act, nay you participate in decisions regarding two
properties osned by th; aity of Irwindale? Parcel A is within 300
ieet of your propeity and Parcel B is in excess of 2,500 feet from
your property.
CONCLUS ION
You may not participate in your official 
capacity in any
govern:nental decision regarding either parcel A or B if the
decision will have a foreseeable material financial effect on your
property .
FACTS
You olrn commercial real property in the city of Irwindale.
This includes property on Arrol, Highway, which you lease to
1^ covernment code Sections 81000-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seo. A1l, references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 . Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 3 22.5660


Our File No. A-9■
-372
Page 2
Hopkins Developnent, which in turn leases the property to Jack-in-
the-gox. This property is within 3oo feet from an office building
(parcel A) and is more than 2,500 feet from another office
Uirifaing iparcel B). Both Parcels A and B are owned by the city.2
on Septernber 19, 1991, the city council will be voting on a
Iease and i sale of parcel B. You would like to know if you can
participate in this decision, as well as any future city council
decisions which wilI involve leases of Parcel A.
ANALYSI S
The Act prohibits a public official 
frorn making,
participating in, or using his official 
position to influence a
governrnental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
ias a financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has
a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will' have a rnaterial financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public. generally, on the
officiil 
or a rnenber of his innediate family or on, anong other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investrnent worth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
(b) Any real. Property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or nore.
(c) Any source of incorue, other than, gifts
and other thin Loans by a conmercial leridihg
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public rrithout regard to
official 
status, aggregating tuo hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or nore in value provided to,
2 Yorrr letter stated that both parcel A and B were olrned by
Birtcher Development Conpany, but that Birtcher has quitclained
both parcels to the city. 
The Birtcher comPany retains an
exclusive right to selt parcel B and a managerial contract to
lease office space in Parcel A. Birtcher will receive a
comrnission for any sale or lease of either of these properties.
Your letter also urentioned that, in the past, you did not
participate in any decisions regarding either parcel A or B.
Although parcel B is located nore than 2,500 feet fro:n your
property, you refrained from any decisions because you srere
conierned about a possible rrlinkagerr between parcel A and B since
proceeds fron parcel B were reinvested in parcel A.
During our telephone conversation, you also i.nforrned ne that
Birtcher has never been a source of income to you,


Our Fi■e No. A-9■-372
Page 3
received by or prornised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the tine when the
decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of managetnent.
Section 87103 (a) - (d) .
Since you have an ownership interest in real property !,hich
exceeds 91,Ooo, you are required to disqualify yourself fron
participating in any governmental decision which wiII foreseeably
and materially affect your property.
Foreseeab i l itv
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it vill occur. Certainty is not
required; however, an effect that is merely a possibility is not
reisonabiy foreseeable. 
( Dor'rnev cares v. Doq/nev coFmunitv.
Developrnent corn. (1987) 195 caI.App.3d 983,' In re Thorner (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 198. )
Materialitv
The Commission has adopted several regulations which define
rnaterial financial effect. 
ReguLation 18702 sets forth the
feneraf guidelines for determining whether an official's 
financial
interest-in a decision is 'rnaterialtr as required by Section 87103.
If the official's 
financial interest is directlv involved in the
decision, Regulation L87o2.1 applies to determine nateriality' 
If
the officialTs financial interest is indirectlv affected by the
decision, Regulations La702.2 through 18702.6 apply to deterrnine
uhether the affect of the decision is material.
Regulation La702.3 contains guidelines for Qeteruining. uhen
the eff6ct of a decision is material as to real property which is
indirectly involved in a decision. Regulation r8702.3 states in
pertinent part:
(a) The effect of a decision is material as
to real property in which an officiaL has a direct,
indirect or beneficial, ownership interest (not
including a leasehold interest), if any of the
foltolring apPlies:
(1) The real property in which the
official 
has an interest, or any part of that
real property, is located within a 300 foot
radius of the boundarj,es (or the proposed
boundaries) of the property which is the
subject of the decision, unless the decision


Our Fi■e NO. A-9■-372
Page 4
will have no financial effect upon the
official's 
real property interest.
(2) The decision involves construction
of, or inprovements to, streets, water, sewer,
storn drainage or similar facilities, 
and the
real property in which the official 
has an
interest wiII receive new or substantially
improved services.
(3) The real ProPerty in which the
official 
has an interest is located outside a
radius of 3oo feet and any part of the real
property is located within a radius of 2,500
feet of the boundaries (or the proposed
boundaries) of the proPerty which is the
subject of the decision and the decision wilL
have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect
of :
(A) Ten thousand dollars (S10,000)
or more on the fair market value of the
real. property in which the official 
has
an interesti or
(B) will affect the rental value of
the proPertY bY S1,000 or nore Per 12
nonth period.
(b) The reasonably foreseeable effect of a
decision is not considered uaterial as to .reaI
property in which an official 
has a direct,
indirecl or beneficial interest (not including a
Ieasehold interest), if the real property in which
the official 
has an interest is located entirely
beyond a 2,500 foot radius of the boundaries (or
thl proposed boundaries) of the proPerty which is
the subject of the decision; unless:
(1) There are specific circumstances
regarding the decision, its effect, and the
nature of the real property in which the
official 
has an interest, which nake it
reasonably foreseeable that the fair rnarket
value or the rental value of the real property
in which the official 
has an interest will 
be
affected by the anounts set forth in
subdivisions (a) (3) (A) or (a) (3) (B); and
(2) Either of the following apply:
(A) The effect wi■l not be
:せ
bi[::ti3:1薔
e[::nia:: :1l tie :[::[rせ:::


Our File No. A-9■
-372
Page 5
which are within a 2,500 foot radius of
the boundarj,es of the real property in
which the official 
has an interest; or
(B) There are not at }east 10
properties under separate ownership
within a 2,500 foot radius of the
ProPerty in which the official 
has an
interest .
Your property is ]ocated within 300 feet from parcel A.
Therefore, pursuant to Regulation 18702.3(a)(1), the effect of a
decision on your Property is deerued rnaterial and you may not
participate in any governrnental decisions regarding parcel A, if
the decisions wilI have any financial effect upon your property'
The decisions in question at the present time pertain to a
sale and lease of parcel B, which is located more than 2,500 feet
frorn your property-. Therefore, you must eval'uate the standards in
negu).ation- 18ro2.a (b) to deterrnine if these decisions will have a
rnaterial financial effect on your property.
Under Regulation 18702.3(d), there are a number of factors
which nust be considered in deternining whether the decision wiII
have a financial effect of 91O,OOO or nore on the fair market
value or $1,OOO or more per twelve month period on the rental
value. These factors include, but are not I'inited to:
(1) The proxinity of the property which is
the subject of the decision and the magnitude of
the proposed project or change in use in ' -
retationship to ttre property in which the official
has an interest;
(2) whether it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will affect the developnent potential
or incone producing potential of the property;
(3) In addition to the foregoing, in the case
of residential property, whether it is reasonabJ'y
foreseeable that the decision will result in a
change to the character of the neighborhood
including, but not Iinited to, effects on traffic,
vj.ew, privacy, intensity of use, noise levels, air
eroissions, or sinilar traits of the neighborhood.
rt is incurnbent upon you to consj,der the above factors and to
make a good faith estinate of the financial effect. 
If a lease or
sale of parcel B will have an effect of 510,000 or nore on the
fair rnarket value or will affect the rental value by $1,000 or
nore per twelve nonth period, you may not particiPate in your
official 
capacity in a decision concerning parcel B.


Our File No. A-9■
-372
Page 6
Makinq, Participatino in Makinq or AttenDtino to Influence a
covernmental Decision
A public official 
makes a governmental decision vhenever he
or she votes on a matter, cornmits the city to a course of action,
or enters into any contractuaL agreenent on behalf of the city.
(Regulation 18700 (b).) 
In addition, a public official
parficipates in a governmental decision when, acting within the
authority of his or her position, he or she:
(1) Negotiates, hlithout significant
substantive review' with a governnental entity or
private person regarding the decision; or
(2) Advises or nakes reconnendations to the
decis ion-rnaker, either directly or without
significant intervening substantive review, by:
(A) conducting research or making any
investigation which requires the exercise of
judgrment on the part of the official 
or
designated employee and the purpose of which
is to influence the decision; or
(B) Preparing or presenting any report,
analysis or opinion, orally or in writing,
which reguires the exercise of judgment on the
part of the official 
or designated emPloyee
lnd the Purpose of which is to influence the
decision.
Regulation 18700 (c)
In addition, under Regulation 18700.1(a), a public official
uses his or her offj.cial position to influence a decision if:
..,for the purpose of influencing the decision, the
official 
contacts, or apPears before, or otherwise
attenpts to influence, any nember, officer,
emptoyee or consultant of the agency. Attempts to
influence include, but are not linited to,
appearances or contacts by the official 
on behalf
of a business entity, client, or custoner.
If it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision will have a
naterial financial effect on your property interest, you nust
disqualify yourself fron uraking or participating in naking that
decision. You also nay not use your official 
position to
influence the decis ion.
Participatin<r in a Non-official. Capacitv
Both Regulation 18700(d) (2) and Regulation 18700.1(b) (1)
allow a public official 
to appear, as any :nember of the general


Our Fi■e No. A-9■-372
Page 7
public, before an agency in the course of its prescribed
governmental function, to represent hinself or herself on matters
related so1ely to his or her personal interests. 
A rrpersonal
interesttt includes an interest in real property which is wholly
olrned by the official 
or menbers of his or her immediate family.
(Regulation 1870o.1(b) (1) (A).)
Regulation 18700.1(b) (2) further aIlows a disqualified public
official 
to comnunicate with the general public or the press.
Thus, the Act allows a public official to comment, as a nember of
the public, at public neetings, foruns, hearings or to the press
on ilsues related solely to the effect of a decision on his
property. once the official 
has fornally disqualified hi:nseJ-f by
announcing his disqua I i f ication and the reason therefore on the
public record (Regulation 18700(b) (5)), any comroents should be
made under the sane procedures applicable to conments from nenbers
of the public. 
For example, you are required to address the city
council fron the audience. Regulations 18700 and 18700.1 permit
you to testify concerning the effects of the proposed decision on
your property, but you nay not testify on behalf of any other
person or group.
I trust this has answered your question. If you have any
further questiOns322::5:llg thiS matter′
 please do not hesitate to
call me at (9■
6)

Scott HaIIabrin
Acting General counsel

commission advice letters nay be obtained from the Corunission at a
cost of 10C per page.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the Act, nay you participate in decisions regarding two
properties osned by th; aity of Irwindale? Parcel A is within 300
ieet of your propeity and Parcel B is in excess of 2,500 feet from
your property.

CONCLUSION: You may not participate in your official 
capacity in any
govern:nental decision regarding either parcel A or B if the

ANALYSI S The Act prohibits a public official frorn making, participating in, or using his official position to influence a governrnental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he ias a financial interest. (Section 87100.) A public official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will' have a rnaterial financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public. generally, on the officiil or a rnenber of his innediate family or on, anong other things: (a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investrnent worth one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
